title: Nested block-comment closer inside <# .SYNOPSIS #> help block
description: PowerShell does not support nested block comments. Any '#>' token inside an outer '<# ... #>' help block (typical when documenting code samples that include '<# ... #>') prematurely closes the outer block. The remainder of the doc text is then parsed as code, producing 'Unexpected token' errors that often point to a line far below the real cause.
fix_pattern: Replace inner '<# ... #>' with prose markers like '(intentional non-fatal)' or use line-comments (#) only inside synopsis blocks. If a nested block must be shown, escape with backtick: `<`#`...`#`> in the help text, or use a code fence in adjacent .md docs instead.
citations: PowerShell language spec: comment tokens are not nestable
